source_refs.html: /Users/toy/English/躺着学/躺着学阅读/8210_Passage 3 Learning Lessons From the Past.html

passages.title: Learning Lessons From the Past
passages.source_html:
Learning Lessons From the Past 


A 


Many past societies collapsed or vanished, leaving behind monumental ruins such as those that the poet Shelley imagined in his sonnet, Ozymandias. By collapse, I mean a drastic decrease in human population size and/or political/economic/social complexity, over a considerable, for an extended time. By those standards, most people would consider the following past societies to have been famous victims of full-fledged collapses rather than of just minor declines: the Anasazi and Cahokia within the boundaries of the modem US, the Maya cities in Central American, Moche and Tiwanaku societies in South America, Norse Greenland, Mycenean Greece and Minoan Crete in Europe, Great Zimbabwe in Africa, Angkor Wat and the Harappan Indus Valley cities in Asia, and Easter Island in the Pacific Ocean. 


B 


The monumental ruins left behind by those past societies hold a fascination for all of us. We marvel at them when as children we first learn of them through pictures. When we grow up, many of us plan vacations in order to experience them at first hand. We feel drawn to their often spectacular and haunting beauty, and also to the mysteries that they pose. The scales of the ruins testify to the former wealth and power of their builders. Yet these builders vanished, abandoning the great structures that they had created at such effort. How could a society that was once so mighty end up collapsing? 


C 


It has long been suspected that many of those mysterious abandonments were at least partly triggered by ecological problems: people inadvertently destroying the environmental resources on which their societies depended. This suspicion of unintended ecological suicide (ecocide) has been confirmed by discoveries made in recent decades by archaeologists, climatologists, historians, palaeontologists, and palynologists (pollen scientists). The processes through which past societies have undermined themselves by damaging their environments fall into eight categories, whose relative importance differs from case to case: deforestation and habitat destruction, soil problems, water management problems, overhunting, overfishing, effects of introduced species on native species, human population growth, and increased impact of people. 


D 


Those past collapses tended to follow somewhat similar courses constituting variations on a theme. Writers find it tempting to draw analogies between the course of human societies and the course of individual human lives – to talk of a society’s birth, growth, peak, old age and eventual death. But that metaphor proves erroneous for many past societies: they declined rapidly after reaching peak numbers and power, and those rapid declines must have come as a surprise and shock to their citizens. Obviously, too, this trajectory is not one that all past societies followed unvaryingly to completion: different societies collapsed to different degrees and in somewhat different ways, while many societies did not collapse at all. 


E 


Today many people feel that environmental problems overshadow all the other threats to global civilisation. These environmental problems include the same eight that undermined past societies, plus four new ones: human-caused climate change, the build-up of toxic chemicals in the environment, energy shortages, and full human utilisation of the Earth’s photosynthetic capacity. But the seriousness of these current environmental problems is vigorously debated. Are the risks greatly exaggerated, or conversely are they underestimated? Will modem technology solve our problems, or is it creating new problems faster than it solves old ones? When we deplete one resource (e.g. wood, oil, or ocean fish), can we count on being able to substitute some new resource (e.g. plastics, wind and solar energy, or farmed fish)? Isn’t the rate of human population growth declining, such that we’re already on course for the world’s population to level off at home manageable number of people?


F 


Questions like this illustrate why those famous collapses of past civilisations have taken on more meaning than just that of a romantic mystery. Perhaps there are some practical lessons that we could learn from all those past collapses. But there are also differences between the modem world and its problems, and those past societies and their problems. We shouldn’t be so naive as to think that the study of the past will yield simple solutions, directly transferable to our societies today. We differ from past societies in some respects that put us at lower risk than them; some of those respects often mentioned include our powerful technology (i.e. its beneficial effects), globalisation, modem medicine, and greater knowledge of past societies and of distant modem societies. We also differ from past societies in some respects that put us at greater risk than them: again, our potent technology (i.e. its unintended destructive effects), globalisation (such that now a problem in one part of the world affects all the rest), the dependence of millions of us on modern medicine for our survival, and our much larger human population. Perhaps we can still learn from the past, but only if we think carefully about its lessons.

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27-29


Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

 


27

When the writer describes the impact of monumental ruins today, he emphasizes

正确答案： D. their romantic appeal.

原文答案句：The monumental ruins left behind by those past societies hold a fascination for all of us. We marvel at them when as children we first learn of them through pictures. When we grow up, many of us plan vacations in order to experience them at first hand. We feel drawn to their often spectacular and haunting beauty, and also to the mysteries that they pose. 

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是作者描述当今宏伟遗迹的影响时，他更侧重哪一点。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"monumental ruins today"和"impact"可以定位到B段，该段描述了宏伟遗迹对现代人的影响。

解题思路：B段中作者描述了宏伟遗迹对现代人的吸引力，包括人们对它们的着迷、惊叹，以及人们被它们的壮丽和神秘所吸引。作者强调了这些遗迹的浪漫吸引力，即它们所呈现的神秘和壮丽之美，以及它们所引发的好奇心。因此，正确答案是D，即它们的浪漫吸引力。


28

Recent findings concerning vanished civilisations

正确答案： C. come from a variety of disciplines.

原文答案句：This suspicion of unintended ecological suicide (ecocide) has been confirmed by discoveries made in recent decades by archaeologists, climatologists, historians, palaeontologists, and palynologists (pollen scientists).

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问关于消失的文明，最近的发现了什么。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的"Recent findings"和"vanished civilisations"定位到C段首句，该句提到"recent decades"的发现与消失的文明相关。

解题思路：C段首句提到"recent decades"的发现由考古学家、气候学家、历史学家、古生物学家和花粉科学家共同证实了消失的文明的"无意间的生态自杀"，说明这些发现来自多个学科领域，对应选项C。选项A、B、D与原文信息不符。


29

What does the writer say about ways in which former societies collapsed?

正确答案： A. The pace of decline was usually similar.

原文答案句：Those past collapses tended to follow somewhat similar courses constituting variations on a theme.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问作者是怎么论述古代社会的衰落方式。

定位关键信息：通过题目的"ways in which former societies collapsed"定位到文章中描述过去社会崩溃方式的句子。

解题思路：原文D段提到"Those past collapses tended to follow somewhat similar courses constituting variations on a theme"，意思是过去的文明衰落通常遵循相似的进程，只是具体细节有所不同。这与选项A"衰落的过程通常相似"意思一致。选项B、C、D在原文中均未提及或与原文表述不符。


Questions 30-34 


Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer in the passage? Write YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer, NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer, NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

 


30

It is widely believed that environmental problems represent the main danger faced by the modern world.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：Today many people feel that environmental problems overshadow all the other threats to global civilisation.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问“人们普遍认为环境问题是现代社会面临的主要危险”是否符合作者的观点。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"environmental problems"和"main danger"定位到E段第一句。

解题思路：段落E中，作者明确写道：“如今，许多人觉得环境问题使全球文明面临的所有其他威胁都相形见绌。”“使...相形见绌”(overshadow) 意味着环境问题被认为比其他威胁更重要、更突出，这与“主要危险”(main danger) 的含义一致。因此，该陈述与作者在文中的信息一致，答案为YES。

31

The accumulation of poisonous substances is a relatively modern problem.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：These environmental problems include the same eight that undermined past societies, plus four new ones: human-caused climate change, the build-up of toxic chemicals in the environment, energy shortages, and full human utilisation of the Earth’s photosynthetic capacity.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是“有毒物质的积累是一个相对现代才出现的问题”是否与作者的观点一致。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“accumulation of poisonous substances”定位到原文E段，找到“the build-up of toxic chemicals in the environment”这一表述。

解题思路：原文E段明确指出有毒化学物质在环境中的积累是四个新的环境问题之一，与过去的八个问题并列，这表明有毒物质的积累是一个相对现代的问题。因此，题目陈述与作者的观点一致，答案为A（Yes）。


32

There is general agreement that the threats posed by environmental problems are very serious.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：But the seriousness of these current environmental problems is vigorously debated. 

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是“人们普遍认为，环境问题带来的威胁非常严重”这一观点是否与作者一致。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"environmental problems"和"very serious"，在文章中定位到E段，该段提到"the seriousness of these current environmental problems is vigorously debated"，意思是当前环境问题的严重性存在激烈争论。

解题思路：根据原文句子"the seriousness of these current environmental problems is vigorously debated"，可以看出作者认为环境问题的严重性存在争议，并没有达成普遍共识，因此题目中"there is general agreement"的说法与原文不符，作者并未表示普遍认同环境问题威胁非常严重。所以答案为No。


33

Some past societies resembled present-day societies more closely than others.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：We differ from past societies in some respects that put us at lower risk than them; some of those respects often mentioned include our powerful technology (i.e. its beneficial effects), globalisation, modem medicine, and greater knowledge of past societies and of distant modem societies. We also differ from past societies in some respects that put us at greater risk than them: again, our potent technology (i.e. its unintended destructive effects), globalisation (such that now a problem in one part of the world affects all the rest), the dependence of millions of us on modern medicine for our survival, and our much larger human population. 

对应段落：F段

审题：题目判断“某些古代社会比其它古代社会更接近现代社会”这一说法是否与作者在文中的观点一致。

定位关键信息：使用 "past societies", "present-day societies", "resembled...more closely" 作为关键词，寻找文中比较不同古代社会与现代社会的相似度的内容。

解题思路：文章确实比较了古代社会和现代社会的异同（主要在段落F），但这是将“古代社会”作为一个整体与“现代社会”进行对比。文中没有任何地方对不同古代社会（如玛雅、复活节岛、格陵兰岛 Norse 社会等）进行相互比较，并评判哪个更接近现代社会。题目信息在原文中找不到依据，因此答案为NOT GIVEN。


34

We should be careful when drawing comparisons between past and present.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：We shouldn’t be so naive as to think that the study of the past will yield simple solutions, directly transferable to our societies today.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“在比较过去和现在时，我们要谨慎。”是否与作者的观点一致。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"comparisons between past and present"可以定位到文章的F段，这一段讨论了研究过去和现在之间的差异，以及我们不应该过于简单地认为过去的研究可以直接应用到现代社会。

解题思路：原文中F段明确提到"我们不应该过于天真地认为，对过去的研究，能给我们现在的问题带来一个简单答案，并且直接把过去的方法搬到现在来。"这表明作者认为在比较过去和现在时，应该谨慎。因此，题目中的陈述与作者的观点一致，应该选择A（Yes）。


Questions 35-39


Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-F, below. Write the correct letter, A-F.


A is not necessarily valid. 

B provides grounds for an optimistic outlook. 

C exists in the form of physical structures. 

D is potentially both positive and negative. 

E will not provide direct solutions for present problems. 

F is greater now than in the past. 

 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
35

35 Evidence of the greatness of some former civilisations
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：We feel drawn to their often spectacular and haunting beauty, and also to the mysteries that they pose. The scales of the ruins testify to the former wealth and power of their builders.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是"某些古老文明曾辉煌的佐证"是什么样的。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"evidence of the greatness of some former civilisations"可以定位到B段中描述古老文明证据的句子。

解题思路：原文中提到，"The scales of the ruins testify to the former wealth and power of their builders"，即"这些废墟的规模证明了他们建造者的昔日的财富和权力"。这句话表明，某些古老文明曾辉煌的佐证，是以实体建筑的形式存在的，即"physical structures"，因此答案为C。
36

36 The parallel between an individual’s life and the life of a society
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Writers find it tempting to draw analogies between the course of human societies and the course of individual human lives – to talk of a society’s birth, growth, peak, old age and eventual death.  But that metaphor proves erroneous for many past societies: they declined rapidly after reaching peak numbers and power, and those rapid declines must have come as a surprise and shock to their citizens.

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问的是，将个体与社会进行类比是怎么样的。  

定位关键信息：通过题干中的"parallel between an individual’s life and the life of a society"定位到D段中"draw analogies between the course of human societies and the course of individual human lives"。  

解题思路：原文明确提到作者们倾向于将社会的发展过程与个体的生命历程进行类比，包括出生、成长、顶峰、老年和死亡。但在很多层面上，这种类比是有误的。也就是说不一定成立，与A项一致，因此答案为A。
37

37 The number of environmental problems that societies face
✅ 正确答案：F

原文答案句：These environmental problems include the same eight that undermined past societies, plus four new ones: human-caused climate change, the build-up of toxic chemicals in the environment, energy shortages, and full human utilisation of the Earth’s photosynthetic capacity.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是社会面临的环境问题是怎么样的。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"environmental problems"和"number"可以定位到E段，该段提到了环境问题的数量。

解题思路：原文中明确提到了环境问题的数量，包括过去社会面临的环境问题和新增的环境问题，总共是八个旧问题加上四个新问题，因此可以确定答案为F。
38

38 The power of technology
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：We differ from past societies in some respects that put us at lower risk than them; some of those respects often mentioned include our powerful technology (i.e. its beneficial effects), globalisation, modem medicine, and greater knowledge of past societies and of distant modem societies. We also differ from past societies in some respects that put us at greater risk than them: again, our potent technology (i.e. its unintended destructive effects), globalisation (such that now a problem in one part of the world affects all the rest), the dependence of millions of us on modern medicine for our survival, and our much larger human population. 

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“科技的力量”怎么样。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的“power of technology”可以定位到F段中提到的“our powerful technology (i.e. its beneficial effects)”。

解题思路：原文中明确指出，我们与过去社会的不同之处在于我们的科技力量，然后说明科技有益的影响，这使得我们相对于过去社会来说风险更低。同时，我们的科技，也会使得现代社会比过去风险更高，然后说明科技的负面影响。因此，科技有利也有弊，因此，选择D项。
39

39 A consideration of historical events and trends
✅ 正确答案：E

原文答案句：We shouldn’t be so naive as to think that the study of the past will yield simple solutions, directly transferable to our societies today.  

对应段落：F段  

审题：题目问的是“对历史事件和趋势的思考”怎么样。  

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"historical events and trends"可以定位到文章中讨论历史事件和趋势的部分。  

解题思路：段落F中，作者明确指出，我们不应该过于天真地认为，对过去的研究，能给我们现在的问题带来一个简单答案，并且直接把过去的方法搬到现在来。这表明作者认为，从历史中学习“不会为当前问题提供直接的解决方案”。这与选项E的含义完全吻合。因此，正确答案是E。

Question 40


Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

 


40

40 What is the main argument of the reading passage?

正确答案： A. There are differences as well as similarities between past and present societies.

原文答案句：Questions like this illustrate why those famous collapses of past civilisations have taken on more meaning than just that of a romantic mystery. Perhaps there are some practical lessons that we could learn from all those past collapses. But there are also differences between the modem world and its problems, and those past societies and their problems... Perhaps we can still learn from the past, but only if we think carefully about its lessons.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是这篇文章的主要论点是什么。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的"main argument"定位到文章中总结性的段落，通常在文章的结尾处。

解题思路：原文F段开始就说明我们或许可以从过去学到一些东西，然后比较现代社会和过去社会的差异，最后一句总结"Perhaps we can still learn from the past, but only if we think carefully about its lessons."（也许我们仍然可以从过去中学习，但前提是必须认真思考其中的教训。）这句话表明文章的主要论点是过去与现在有相似，也有不同，这与选项A中提到的“过去和现在社会之间既有差异也有相似之处”是一致的。因此，选项A最符合文章的主要论点。
